title: calibrachoa missionica
intro: Calibrachoa missionica, a type of flowering plant native to South America, belongs to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have potential benefits in treating skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. The health benefits of Calibrachoa missionica include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection. In herbal preparations, Calibrachoa missionica is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
